response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced polyglot engineer (currently Staff SWE at Google) with genuine low-latency and high-throughput systems exposure, but he falls far short of the core minimum qualifications for this role: his professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments versus the required 8 years, his infrastructure work is mostly web/product-oriented rather than cloud compute platform engineering, and he has never held a formal technical-leadership role owning cross-team strategy, priorities, and deliverables.
strengths: Broad, current engineering experience at top-tier companies, including Staff Software Engineer at Google
Real high-throughput, low-latency systems work (microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Strong testing and mentoring culture: TDD/CI/CD craftsmanship, open-source test tooling, workshops and courses
concerns: C++ experience (~7 months professional) is drastically below the 8-year minimum requirement
Little evidence of building or operating large-scale cloud compute platforms or production cloud architecture
No formal technical-lead role; leadership is limited to workshops and knowledge-sharing rather than owning team strategy and deliverables

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud platform / large-scale infrastructure depth
Strong general engineering pedigree and distributed systems exposure
